title: AccountRepository + AccountUoW
description:
Implement AccountRepository following MediaBuyRepository pattern. Add AccountUoW to uow.py. Methods: get_by_id, get_by_natural_key, list_for_agent, upsert_from_sync, update_status, deactivate_missing, grant_access, has_access, resolve_reference.

Files: src/core/database/repositories/account.py, src/core/database/repositories/uow.py
design:
## Core Invariant
All account data access goes through AccountRepository with mandatory tenant_id scoping — no raw select(Account) or session.add(Account) outside the repository.

## Architecture Decisions
1. Single repository file with AccountRepository handling both Account and AgentAccountAccess (following CreativeRepository pattern)
2. AccountUoW with single accounts attribute (AccountRepository handles access table internally)
3. get_by_natural_key queries operator + brand JSON column — uses PostgreSQL JSONB operators

## Implementation Plan
1. Create src/core/database/repositories/account.py with AccountRepository:
   - __init__(session, tenant_id)
   - get_by_id(account_id) -> Account | None
   - get_by_natural_key(operator, brand_domain, brand_id=None) -> Account | None
   - list_for_agent(principal_id) -> list[Account] (joins AgentAccountAccess)
   - list_all(*, status=None) -> list[Account]
   - create(account: Account) -> Account (flush, not commit)
   - update_status(account_id, status) -> Account | None
   - update_fields(account_id, **kwargs) -> Account | None
   - grant_access(principal_id, account_id) -> AgentAccountAccess
   - revoke_access(principal_id, account_id) -> bool
   - has_access(principal_id, account_id) -> bool
   - list_accessible_account_ids(principal_id) -> list[str]
   - resolve_reference(ref: AccountReference) -> Account | None
2. Add AccountUoW to uow.py following MediaBuyUoW pattern
3. Register in repositories/__init__.py
4. Integration tests in tests/integration/test_account_repository.py
notes:
## Findings

### Repository Pattern (from MediaBuyRepository)
- Constructor: (session: Session, tenant_id: str), stored as _session/_tenant_id
- All queries include tenant_id scoping
- Writes: session.add() + flush(), never commit (UoW's job)
- _IMMUTABLE_FIELDS frozenset guards PKs/audit columns
- Methods return ORM models or None

### UoW Pattern (from BaseUoW in uow.py)
- __init__(tenant_id), __enter__ calls get_db_session(), __exit__ commits
- Abstract _init_repos() and _clear_repos()
- CreativeUoW has 2 repos (CreativeRepository + CreativeAssignmentRepository) — analogous to Account + AgentAccountAccess

### Junction Table Pattern (CreativeAssignmentRepository)
- get_by_creative, get_by_media_buy, get_existing (composite key)
- create(**kwargs), delete(id) — for AgentAccountAccess: grant/revoke/has_access

### Relevant Code
- src/core/database/repositories/media_buy.py — reference implementation
- src/core/database/repositories/uow.py — BaseUoW + all concrete UoWs
- src/core/database/repositories/creative.py:322-477 — junction table pattern
- src/core/database/repositories/__init__.py — exports
- tests/integration/test_tenant_config_repository.py — test pattern (factories + IntegrationEnv)

### Test Pattern
- _RepoEnv(IntegrationEnv) with EXTERNAL_PATCHES = {}
- Create data with factories, get session, construct repo, assert
- Test categories: happy path, tenant isolation, empty results, write roundtrip
## Architect Review
- [LOW] get_by_natural_key JSONB query: brand is JSONType. Use PostgreSQL JSON operators (brand->>'domain') for the query. This is well-supported by SQLAlchemy.
- [LOW] resolve_reference needs to handle RootModel AccountReference. Extract account_id from variant 1, or (brand, operator) from variant 2. Use existing adcp accessor pattern.
- [LOW] _IMMUTABLE_FIELDS should include tenant_id, account_id, created_at.

Overall: ALL_LOW
## Triage
Clean review (ALL_LOW) — proceeding to implement.
## Regression Test
- Test: tests/integration/test_account_repository.py (5 test classes, 6 tests)
- Status: FAILING (ModuleNotFoundError — repository not yet created)
- Tests: get_by_id, tenant isolation, list_for_agent, grant/has_access, UoW lifecycle
close_reason: AccountRepository + AccountUoW implemented with 6 integration tests

title: Replace ValueError/RuntimeError with AdCPAdapterError in creative_agent_registry.py
description:
PR #1167 review (ChrisHuie): creative_agent_registry.py raises bare ValueError/RuntimeError for agent misbehavior, but callers only catch ValueError — RuntimeError escapes unhandled.

## What needs to change

5 sites in src/core/creative_agent_registry.py currently raise ValueError or RuntimeError when a creative agent returns bad/unexpected responses. These should all become AdCPAdapterError (502, transient recovery) since they represent agent transport/protocol failures, not business logic errors.

| Line (approx) | Current | New | Trigger |
|---|---|---|---|
| ~299 | ValueError('Completed status but no data') | AdCPAdapterError | Agent returned completed with null data |
| ~318 | ValueError('Unexpected submitted status') | AdCPAdapterError | Sync call got async response |
| ~349 | ValueError('Unexpected result status') | AdCPAdapterError | Unknown status enum |
| ~445 | RuntimeError('No parseable result in MCP response') | AdCPAdapterError | Raw MCP fallback got unparseable response |
| ~459 | RuntimeError('No text content in MCP tool result') | AdCPAdapterError | MCP result missing text content |

## Implementation notes

- Check .agent-index/errors.pyi FIRST for AdCPAdapterError signature
- Use ast-grep (not grep) for structural queries to find raise sites
- Add import: from src.core.exceptions import AdCPAdapterError
- Existing RuntimeError raises for ADCPAuthenticationError/ADCPTimeoutError/ADCPConnectionError re-raises (lines ~355-365) should stay as-is — those are already caught by the outer except blocks
- Run make quality after changes

title: Phase 1.2: Wire UC-002 create_media_buy BDD steps + shared step modules
description:
Create step definitions and TWO test modules for UC-002 create_media_buy:

Step definitions (shared by both integration and e2e):
- tests/bdd/steps/domain/uc002_create_media_buy.py — When steps specific to create
- tests/bdd/steps/generic/given_media_buy.py — shared Given steps (tenant config, account setup, media buy state, request construction)
- tests/bdd/steps/generic/then_media_buy.py — shared Then steps (media_buy_id, packages, error recovery, persistence checks)

Test modules:
- tests/bdd/test_uc002_create_media_buy.py — integration: parametrize [impl, a2a, mcp, rest], uses MediaBuyEnv
- tests/e2e/test_uc002_create_media_buy.py — e2e: parametrize [mcp, a2a, rest], uses MediaBuyE2EEnv, needs Docker stack

Both import the SAME feature file (tests/bdd/features/BR-UC-002-create-media-buy.feature, 70 scenarios) and the SAME step definitions. The only difference is the conftest fixture providing ctx['env'].

Wire enough steps to graduate main-flow and alt-flow scenarios. ~80 new Given steps, ~15 new When steps, ~50 new Then steps. Depends on: harness task (salesagent-4n0).
design:
## Core Invariant
Same Gherkin, same step definitions, different env fixture — steps must be transport-agnostic and never import transport-specific code. Steps write to ctx; dispatch reads ctx['transport'] and routes through env.call_via().

## Implementation Plan

### Phase A: Extend conftest.py for UC-002 create flows
1. Add MediaBuyCreateEnv to _harness_env fixture for UC-002 non-account scenarios
2. Register new step modules in pytest_plugins list
3. No new test module needed — existing test_uc002_create_media_buy.py already calls scenarios()

### Phase B: Given steps (tests/bdd/steps/generic/given_media_buy.py)
Build a request-construction layer using factory pattern:
1. 'a valid create_media_buy request' → ctx['request_kwargs'] with factory defaults
2. 'a valid create_media_buy request with:' (table) → merge table overrides into ctx['request_kwargs']
3. Tenant config steps: auto-approval, human_review_required, max_daily_package_spend
4. Package construction: product_ids, budgets, pricing_option_ids, currencies
5. Error injection: 'But X' steps override ctx['request_kwargs'] with invalid values
6. Adapter state: 'adapter is available', 'adapter returns error'
7. Media buy state: 'a media buy exists in X state' → factory-create in DB via env

### Phase C: When steps (extend uc002_create_media_buy.py)
1. 'the Buyer Agent sends the create_media_buy request' → build CreateMediaBuyRequest from ctx['request_kwargs'] → dispatch_request(ctx, req=req)
2. Result storage: dispatch_request already stores in ctx['response'] or ctx['error']

### Phase D: Then steps (tests/bdd/steps/generic/then_media_buy.py)
1. Response assertions: status, media_buy_id, packages, workflow_step_id
2. Error assertions: error_code, recovery, suggestion, message (extend existing then_error.py where possible)
3. DB persistence checks: 'media buy record should be persisted' → query DB via env
4. Notification checks: 'Slack notification should be sent' → check mock

### Phase E: Wire conftest xfails
1. Add _XFAIL_TAGS entries for scenarios that can't pass yet (e.g., proposal-based, creative upload)
2. Remove xfails as steps become functional

### Key Patterns
- Request construction: ctx['request_kwargs'] dict built incrementally by Given steps, assembled into CreateMediaBuyRequest in When step
- Use dispatch_request(ctx, req=req) from _dispatch.py — NOT custom _call()
- Reuse existing then_success.py and then_error.py for generic assertions
- New then_media_buy.py only for create-specific assertions (packages, persistence)
- Adapter mock injection via env.mock['adapter'] — already set up in harness
notes:
## Research Findings (2026-03-21)

### Feature File: BR-UC-002-create-media-buy.feature
- 56 scenarios (42 regular + 14 outlines → ~170+ parameterized cases)
- Only account resolution slice currently wired (MediaBuyAccountEnv)
- Everything else auto-xfailed at runtime (KeyError on ctx['env'])
- Flows: 1 main, 5 alt, 22+ exception, 21 invariant, 14 partition outlines, 14 boundary outlines, 4 NFR, 3 sandbox

### Production Code: media_buy_create.py (~3900 lines)
- _create_media_buy_impl is ASYNC (needs asyncio.run in harness)
- Signature: (req: CreateMediaBuyRequest, push_notification_config, identity, context_id) -> CreateMediaBuyResult
- CreateMediaBuyResult contains status + response (Success|Error), supports tuple unpacking
- Validation errors: RETURNED as CreateMediaBuyError (not raised) via ValueError/PermissionError catch
- Auth/adapter errors: RAISED as AdCPError subclasses
- Key validation chain: identity → setup → buyer_ref → budget → datetime → product → currency → pricing → min_spend → daily_cap → targeting → creative → approval → adapter → persist

### Dispatch Pattern
- Use generic dispatch_request(ctx, **kwargs) from _dispatch.py (like UC-004/UC-011)
- NOT the UC-005-specific _call()/_call_via() pattern
- dispatch_request reads ctx['transport'], delegates to env.call_via(transport, **kwargs)
- MCP dispatch does model_dump(exclude_none=True) for flat kwargs

### Harness: MediaBuyCreateEnv
- EXTERNAL_PATCHES: adapter, audit, slack, context_mgr
- call_impl: asyncio.run(_create_media_buy_impl(req=req, identity=identity))
- call_a2a: asyncio.run(create_media_buy_raw(**kwargs))
- call_mcp: _run_mcp_wrapper(create_media_buy, CreateMediaBuyResult, **kwargs)
- REST_ENDPOINT: /api/v1/media-buys
- Mock adapter defaults: {platform_order_id: 'mock_order_001', platform_line_item_ids: {}}

### Factories Available
- MediaBuyFactory: ORM model (status=pending_approval, budget=10000, USD, dates 2025-2027)
- MediaPackageFactory: ORM model (budget=5000, pacing=even)
- TenantFactory, PrincipalFactory: via SubFactory

### conftest.py Transport Parametrization
- pytest_generate_tests parametrizes [IMPL, A2A, MCP, REST] for all ctx-using scenarios
- _IMPL_ONLY: (UC-002, account) currently — must NOT add create to this set
- Auto-xfail on StepDefinitionNotFoundError, KeyError('env'), NotImplementedError
- _harness_env fixture: UC-002 currently only handles @account tag → MediaBuyAccountEnv

### Step File Structure Needed
- tests/bdd/steps/domain/uc002_create_media_buy.py — Already exists (account resolution)
- tests/bdd/steps/generic/given_media_buy.py — NEW: shared Given steps
- tests/bdd/steps/generic/then_media_buy.py — NEW: shared Then steps
- Existing generic: given_auth.py, then_success.py, then_error.py already cover some patterns

### Risks
1. CreateMediaBuyRequest construction is complex (many required fields)
2. _impl is async — all transports need asyncio.run or async support
3. Validation returns errors (not raises) — Then steps must check ctx['response'] not ctx['error'] for some failures
4. The feature file has VERY specific Then steps (~66 unique patterns) — many are specialized
## Architect Review (2026-03-21)

### Findings

1. [MEDIUM] conftest.py routing: needs else-branch for non-account UC-002 → MediaBuyCreateEnv. Clear 6-line change but env needs richer data setup.

2. [LOW] test module exists: test_uc002_create_media_buy.py already calls scenarios(). No work needed.

3. [LOW] dispatch_request handles async: env.call_impl wraps asyncio.run() internally. No action.

4. [HIGH] When step conflict: existing when_send_create_media_buy calls resolve_account_or_error(ctx), not dispatch_request(ctx). Same step text cannot serve both account-resolution and full create flows. Options: (a) type-check env in step body, (b) rename account-specific step, (c) new step text for non-account flows.

5. [HIGH] Request construction viable but When step dispatch needs fixing first. ctx['request_kwargs'] accumulation pattern is correct (matches UC-004).

6. [HIGH] setup_default_data() insufficient: no CurrencyLimit, PropertyTag, Products created. MediaBuyCreateEnv needs setup_media_buy_data() with full dependency chain. Without this, happy-path scenarios fail on budget/product validation before reaching business logic.

7. [HIGH] Scope: 112 scenarios × 4 transports. Recommended decomposition: Phase 1 (harness wiring), Phase 2 (core + ext scenarios), Phase 3 (partitions/invariants). All 112 in one task is unrealistic.

### Overall: NEEDS_REFINEMENT

The 3 HIGH findings can be resolved without user input:
- Finding 4: Use approach (a) — type-check env in When step. If MediaBuyCreateEnv, dispatch through harness; if MediaBuyAccountEnv, use existing account resolution path. This is the same pattern UC-004 uses for delivery vs webhook envs.
- Finding 6: Add setup_media_buy_data() to MediaBuyCreateEnv that creates Products+CurrencyLimit+PropertyTag+PricingOption. Call from conftest.py.
- Finding 7: Focus this task on main flow + alt flows + extension scenarios (28 scenarios). File follow-up tasks for partitions/invariants.
## Triage (2026-03-21)
Verdict: NEEDS_REFINEMENT → resolved autonomously.
- When step routing: type-check env in step body
- DB setup: add setup_media_buy_data() on MediaBuyCreateEnv
- Scope: main + alt + extension (28 scenarios), file follow-ups for partitions/invariants
## Regression Test
- Test: tests/bdd/test_uc002_create_media_buy.py::test_autoapproved_media_buy_with_valid_packagebased_request[impl]
- Status: XFAIL (auto-xfail, no env set — KeyError on ctx['env'])
- Tests expected behavior: main flow auto-approved create_media_buy should succeed through all 4 transports
- Target: 28 scenarios (main + alt + ext) × 4 transports = 112 test cases should PASS after implementation
## Implementation Summary (2026-03-21)

### Delivered
- MediaBuyCreateEnv.setup_media_buy_data() — creates full dependency chain (tenant+CurrencyLimit+PropertyTag+PublisherPartner+Product+PricingOption)
- Fixed adapter mock to return real CreateMediaBuySuccess with dynamic package generation
- Fixed MCP/A2A/REST transport dispatch for create_media_buy (unpack req, exclude unsupported fields, fix response parsing)
- conftest.py routes non-@account UC-002 scenarios to MediaBuyCreateEnv
- Given steps: 15+ new steps for request construction, package setup, error injection, tenant config
- Then steps: 12+ new steps for response assertions, persistence checks, notifications
- When step: dual dispatch (account-resolution vs full create) based on env type
- Error result promotion: CreateMediaBuyResult with failed status → ctx['error']

### Results
- Main flow (T-UC-002-main): 4/4 transports PASS (impl, a2a, mcp, rest)
- Extension scenarios: step definitions wired but some have spec-production gaps (e.g., BUDGET_TOO_LOW vs validation_error)
- Alt-manual: needs further work (manual_approval_required propagation)
- Quality gate: 4077 passed, 0 failed (same as baseline)

### Follow-up work needed
- Fix alt-manual scenario (tenant approval flag propagation to adapter)
- Fix extension scenarios where error codes don't match spec expectations
- Wire remaining partition/boundary/invariant scenarios
- Wire alt-asap, alt-creatives, alt-proposal scenarios

title: Research salesagent-4d4
description:
Execute research for beads task salesagent-4d4. All findings go into the
bead itself — NOT the filesystem.

## Instructions

1. **Read the task**: `bd show salesagent-4d4`
2. **AdCP spec check** (if task touches schemas/protocol):
   - Read relevant JSON schemas at `/Users/konst/projects/adcp/static/schemas/source/`
   - Check required vs optional, additionalProperties, type constraints
   - If no AdCP surface, note "N/A" and move on
3. **Explore the codebase**:
   - Search for relevant code (Grep, Glob)
   - Read files that will need modification
   - Check existing tests for the affected area
   - Find 2-3 similar implementations to follow as patterns
4. **Check documentation** (doc-first rule):
   - Use Ref MCP for library docs
   - Check CLAUDE.md for project patterns
5. **Engineering checklist** — answer concretely:
   - DRY: Does similar logic exist?
   - Library idioms: How does Pydantic/SQLAlchemy/FastMCP solve this?
   - Data flow trace: Walk one concrete example through the full chain
   - Existing conventions: What pattern do similar implementations follow?
   - Test infrastructure: What fixtures/helpers exist?
6. **Identify architecture decisions**:
   - What CLAUDE.md patterns apply?
   - Multiple valid approaches?
   - Risks and edge cases?
7. **Extract the core invariant** — State in ONE sentence the architectural
   principle that ALL changes for this task must preserve. This becomes the
   litmus test for every implementation decision.
   - Good: "model_dump() only at framework boundaries, never in business logic"
   - Good: "all pricing validation happens in the Pydantic model, not callers"
   - Bad: "improve the code" (untestable, vague)
   If this task is part of a multi-task plan, the invariant should come from
   the plan's stated intent. Every task in the plan shares this invariant.

## Store Research in the Bead

Write findings to the bead using `bd update`:

```bash
bd update salesagent-4d4 --append-notes "## Findings
- [key findings]

## AdCP Spec Verification
- [schemas checked, field alignment, divergences]

## Relevant Code
- path/to/file.py:line — [description]

## CLAUDE.md Patterns
- [which patterns apply]

## Risks & Edge Cases
- [potential issues]"
```

Write architecture decisions, invariant, and implementation plan to
the design field:

```bash
bd update salesagent-4d4 --design "## Core Invariant
[One sentence: the architectural principle every change must preserve]

## Architecture Decisions
- Decision 1: [rationale]

## Implementation Plan
1. [first step]
2. [second step]"
```

## Gate: Add Label

ONLY after notes AND design are written:
```bash
bd label add salesagent-4d4 research:complete
```

If research reveals blockers or unresolved questions:
```bash
bd label add salesagent-4d4 research:blocked
bd update salesagent-4d4 --append-notes "BLOCKED: [reason]"
```
Do NOT close this atom if blocked — leave it open.

title: Architect review of salesagent-4d4
description:
Pragmatic architect review of the research findings for salesagent-4d4.

## Precondition

Run `bd show salesagent-4d4` and verify:
1. Label `research:complete` is present
2. Notes field is non-empty (contains ## Findings)
3. Design field is non-empty (contains ## Implementation Plan)

If ANY precondition fails: do NOT proceed. Update this atom's notes
explaining what's missing and leave it open.

## Review Criteria

Read the task's notes and design fields. Evaluate:

1. **Invariant alignment**: Does EVERY step of the implementation plan
   maintain the stated Core Invariant? Simulate each change mentally:
   if a step would violate the invariant, that's a HIGH finding.
2. **Approach soundness**: Is the proposed implementation plan technically correct?
   Will it actually work? Are there fundamental flaws?
3. **Risk coverage**: Are the identified risks real? Are there unidentified risks?
   Check for: circular imports, breaking changes, missing test coverage, perf issues.
4. **CLAUDE.md compliance**: Does the plan follow all applicable critical patterns?
   (AdCP schema, nested serialization, shared impl, no quiet failures, etc.)
5. **Specificity**: Is the plan specific enough to implement without guessing?
   Vague plans like "update the code" are HIGH findings.
6. **Alternatives**: Was an obviously better approach missed?

## Rate Each Finding

- **LOW**: Minor suggestion, cosmetic, nice-to-have
- **MEDIUM**: Real concern but agent can resolve autonomously with the suggestion
- **HIGH**: Fundamental issue — user input required before proceeding

## Write Review

```bash
bd update salesagent-4d4 --append-notes "## Architect Review
- [RATING] Finding: [description]. Suggestion: [what to do]
...

Overall: [ALL_LOW | NEEDS_REFINEMENT | NEEDS_USER_INPUT]"
```
acceptance_criteria: Review written to task notes with rated findings and overall verdict.
notes: Architect review: Simple extraction. WorkflowRepository follows DeliveryRepository pattern. WorkflowUoW follows existing UoW pattern. 3 functions migrated, no spec changes. Risk: low.

title: Write regression test for salesagent-4d4
description:
Write a failing test that defines the expected behavior for salesagent-4d4.
This is the TDD "red" step — the test MUST exist and MUST fail before
any implementation begins.

## Prerequisites

Run `bd show salesagent-4d4`. Read:
- **Design**: Core Invariant + Implementation Plan
- **Notes**: Research findings, architect review

## Instructions

1. **Identify what to test**: From the implementation plan, determine
   the key behavior that this task adds or changes. What should be true
   after implementation that isn't true now?

2. **Choose test level — default to integration**:

   **The default test level is integration** (`tests/integration/`). A test
   that runs against a real PostgreSQL database with real ORM models catches
   bugs that mocked unit tests miss entirely.

   Only use unit tests (`tests/unit/`) when ALL of these are true:
   - The function is pure computation (no DB, no I/O, no side effects)
   - The logic being tested is complex enough to warrant a focused test
   - Mocking the DB would make the test meaningless (e.g., the bug IS in
     how data flows through the DB layer)

   If in doubt, write an integration test.

   **Entity-suite-aware placement** (within your chosen level):
   - **First, check for an entity test suite**:
     ```bash
     ls tests/unit/test_product.py tests/unit/test_media_buy.py \
        tests/unit/test_creative.py tests/unit/test_delivery.py \
        tests/unit/test_pricing_option.py tests/unit/test_properties.py \
        tests/unit/test_creative_formats.py tests/unit/test_capabilities.py \
        tests/unit/test_performance.py tests/unit/test_audience_signals.py \
        2>/dev/null
     ```
   - If the affected entity has a suite, add your test THERE as a
     new method in the appropriate test class.
   - If no entity suite exists, note in the task:
     "No entity test suite for {entity}. Consider running /surface {entity}."
   - **Fallback**: `tests/unit/test_<area>.py` (existing file) or
     `tests/integration/test_<area>.py` (if DB-dependent)
   - Follow existing test file organization

3. **Write the failing test**:
   - Test the BEHAVIOR, not the implementation details
   - One test per distinct behavior change
   - Use descriptive test names: `test_<what>_<when>_<expected>`
   - Follow existing test patterns in the same file
   - **Transport Parity Invariant**: Every behavioral test MUST verify
     identical behavior across all three transports (MCP, A2A, REST).
     Use `@pytest.mark.parametrize("transport", ["mcp", "a2a", "rest"])`
     or explicitly test the same scenario through all 3 entry points.
     If behavior differs between transports, the bug is in the wrapper.
     See `docs/development/architecture.md` "Transport Parity Invariant".
   - **Use factories for test data** (CLAUDE.md Pattern #8):
     - Import from `tests.helpers.adcp_factories` — never inline-construct
       ORM models or Pydantic schemas with manual field plucking
     - Available: `create_test_db_product`, `create_test_package_request`,
       `create_test_creative_asset`, `create_test_brand_manifest`,
       `create_test_media_buy_request_dict`, etc.
     - See `tests/helpers/adcp_factories.py` for full list
     - For DB fixtures: use conftest.py fixtures, never inline `session.add()`

4. **Factory check**: Verify your test uses factories:
   ```bash
   uv run python .pre-commit-hooks/check_test_factories.py tests/<level>/test_<area>.py
   ```
   If this reports suggestions for your NEW code, refactor to use factories.

5. **Run and confirm it fails for the RIGHT reason**:
   ```bash
   scripts/run-test.sh tests/<level>/test_<area>.py::test_<name> -x -v
   ```
   - ImportError or NameError = wrong reason (test has a bug)
   - AssertionError with clear message = right reason (behavior doesn't exist yet)
   - If the test already passes: the behavior already exists. Document this
     and consider whether additional edge case tests are needed.

## BANNED Patterns

- **`pytest.mark.xfail` is BANNED.** xfail suppresses failure — the test
  suite stays green. A regression test must produce a HARD FAILURE: pytest
  exit code 1, "FAILED" in the output. xfail is NOT failing.

- **AST-scanning / source-code pattern tests are BANNED.** Regression tests
  must call the actual function (`_impl`, factory, schema constructor) and
  assert behavioral output. If the bug were re-introduced through a
  different code construct, the test must still catch it. Tests that use
  `ast.parse`, `grep`, or regex on source files are structural guards
  (use /guard for those), NOT regression tests.

## Verification: Paste the FAILED Output

After running the test, paste the LAST 5 LINES of pytest output into
the bead notes. You MUST see the word "FAILED" and a non-zero exit.
If you see "passed", "xfailed", or "xpassed", your test is wrong.

```bash
scripts/run-test.sh tests/<level>/test_<area>.py::test_<name> -x -v 2>&1 | tail -5
# You MUST see "FAILED" — if you see "passed", rewrite the test
```

6. **For refactors/rewrites** (behavior should NOT change):
   - Verify existing tests pass BEFORE your changes:
     ```bash
     uv run pytest tests/unit/test_<area>.py -x -v
     ```
   - Write additional tests for edge cases the refactor must preserve
   - These tests should PASS now (they guard existing behavior)

## When to Skip (with justification)

If the task is PURELY dead code removal or comment/doc changes:
```bash
bd update salesagent-4d4 --append-notes "## Regression Test: Skipped
Task is [dead code removal / documentation only] — no behavioral change to test.
Existing tests provide sufficient coverage."
```
Close this atom. But if ANY behavior changes, a test is MANDATORY.

## Store Test Reference

```bash
bd update salesagent-4d4 --append-notes "## Regression Test
- Test: tests/unit/test_<area>.py::test_<name>
- Status: FAILING (expected — TDD red step)
- Tests expected behavior: [one sentence]"
```

## Gate

Do NOT close this atom until:
- A failing test exists, OR
- Skip justification is documented
acceptance_criteria: Failing test exists that defines the expected behavior. Test reference written to task notes. Test fails for the right reason (AssertionError, not ImportError).

title: Implement salesagent-4d4
description:
Implement beads task salesagent-4d4 — make the failing test pass (TDD green),
then refactor.

## Prerequisites

Run `bd show salesagent-4d4`. The bead contains everything you need:
- **Description**: What the task is
- **Notes**: Research findings, architect review, regression test reference
- **Design**: Architecture decisions and implementation plan

Read ALL of these before writing any code.

## Pre-Implementation Checks

1. **Verify the regression test still fails** (from the write-test atom):
   ```bash
   scripts/run-test.sh tests/<level>/test_<area>.py::test_<name> -x -v
   ```
   If the test now passes (e.g., someone else implemented it), note this
   and close the atom.

2. **Invariant check**: Re-read the ## Core Invariant from the design field.
   For each file you plan to modify, ask:
   > "Does this change maintain the invariant?"
   If "no" or "unclear" for any change, STOP and leave this atom open.

## Workflow (TDD Green + Refactor)

1. **Green**: Write minimum code to make the failing test pass
   - Follow CLAUDE.md critical patterns
   - Follow existing codebase conventions found in research
   - Don't add extras not covered by tests

2. **Verify (red → green)**:
   Run the SAME test that FAILED in the write-test atom:
   ```bash
   scripts/run-test.sh tests/<level>/test_<area>.py::test_<name> -x -v 2>&1 | tail -5
   # You MUST see "passed" — if you see "FAILED", your fix didn't work
   ```
   If the test still fails, do NOT mark it xfail and move on. Fix the code.

3. **Refactor**: With passing tests as safety net
   - Remove duplication, improve naming, simplify logic

4. **Quality gate**:
   ```bash
   make quality
   ```

## When EXISTING Tests Fail

If your changes cause pre-existing tests to fail:

1. **STOP. Do NOT modify the failing tests.**
2. Re-read the ## Core Invariant from the design field.
3. Ask: "Do the failing tests indicate my approach violates the invariant?"
4. If YES → your approach is wrong, not the tests. Revert and try a
   different approach. Update task notes explaining what you tried and why
   it violated the invariant.
5. If NO → the test has a genuine bug or the requirements changed.
   Document WHY in task notes BEFORE modifying any test. Explain what the
   test was protecting and why the new behavior is correct.

**Sacred rule**: NEVER adjust tests to match code without first validating
that the test's intent conflicts with the stated invariant.

## If Blocked

If `make quality` fails or requirements are unclear:
```bash
bd update salesagent-4d4 --append-notes "## Implementation Blocked
- [describe the blocker: test failure, unclear requirement, etc.]
- [what was tried]
- [what direction is needed]"
```
Leave this atom OPEN for user direction.

## If Successful

Close the original beads task:
```bash
bd close salesagent-4d4
```
acceptance_criteria: make quality passes. Regression test from write-test atom now passes. No existing tests modified without documented justification. Changes maintain the Core Invariant. Original task salesagent-4d4 closed.

title: Verify zero regressions vs baseline
description:
Compare final test results against the baseline recorded in the setup atom.
This is the clean slate verification — you must return what you received.

## Instructions

1. **Read the baseline** from the epic:
   ```bash
   bd show salesagent-njp
   ```
   Find the "## Baseline (pre-change)" section. Note the exact counts.

2. **Run the full test suite** (MANDATORY — this is the only correct way):
   ```bash
   ./run_all_tests.sh
   ```
   This starts Docker, runs all 5 suites (unit, integration, integration_v2,
   e2e, ui) via tox, and tears down. Do NOT substitute with individual pytest
   commands — `./run_all_tests.sh` is the single source of truth.

   JSON results are saved to `test-results/<ddmmyy_HHmm>/`. If terminal
   output is lost (background process crash, timeout), read the JSON files
   to review results.

3. **Compare counts**:
   - Passes must be >= baseline (new tests are expected)
   - Failures must be exactly 0
   - Any new failure means YOUR changes broke something — fix it

4. **Record final results**:
   ```bash
   bd update salesagent-njp --append-notes "## Final (post-change)
   - make quality: [N] passed, 0 failed (baseline: [N])
   - integration: [N] passed, 0 failed (baseline: [N])
   - integration_v2: [N] passed, 0 failed (baseline: [N])
   - e2e: [N] passed, [N] skipped, 0 failed
   - ui: [N] passed, [N] skipped, 0 failed
   - Delta: +[N] new tests, 0 regressions"
   ```

## HARD STOP — Test Integrity (ZERO TOLERANCE)

**Read CLAUDE.md "Test Integrity Policy". These rules apply here:**

If ANY test fails in `./run_all_tests.sh`:
- Do NOT close this atom.
- Do NOT commit.
- Do NOT use `--ignore`, `-k "not ..."`, or `--deselect` to skip failures.
- Do NOT rationalize failures as "pre-existing", "infrastructure issue",
  "misplaced test", "needs a running server", or "was deselected".
- Fix the regressions first, then re-run `./run_all_tests.sh`.
- If infrastructure is broken and you cannot fix it, report it as a
  BLOCKER to the user. Do NOT skip tests and report success.
acceptance_criteria: All three gates pass. Zero regressions vs baseline. Final counts recorded in epic notes.

title: McpE2EDispatcher + env TOOL_NAME additions for real MCP protocol
description:
## What

Build McpE2EDispatcher that uses fastmcp.Client with StreamableHttpTransport to exercise the full MCP protocol (JSON-RPC framing, tool discovery, parameter dispatch, enum coercion). Add TOOL_NAME and build_mcp_args() to each harness env.

## Why

MCP is the primary buyer-agent protocol. The current MCP dispatcher creates a MagicMock(spec=Context) and calls the wrapper function directly -- bypassing JSON-RPC framing, FastMCP parameter dispatch, enum coercion, and MCPAuthMiddleware. Real MCP E2E exercises all of this.

## Architecture Context

### Current MCP dispatch (in-process):
```
McpDispatcher.dispatch(env) -> env.call_mcp(**kwargs) -> asyncio.run(wrapper_fn(ctx=mock_ctx, **kwargs))
```
The mock_ctx has get_state = AsyncMock(return_value=mcp_identity). FastMCP is completely bypassed.

### New MCP E2E dispatch (real protocol):
```
McpE2EDispatcher.dispatch(env) -> fastmcp.Client.call_tool(TOOL_NAME, args) -> StreamableHttpTransport -> nginx -> FastMCP HTTP app -> MCPAuthMiddleware -> tool dispatch -> _impl -> response
```

### Reference implementation (tests/e2e/test_adcp_full_lifecycle.py:34-139):
Already does real MCP E2E:
```python
transport = StreamableHttpTransport(url=f"{live_server['mcp']}/mcp/", headers=headers)
async with Client(transport=transport) as client:
    result = await client.call_tool("get_products", {"brief": "video ads"})
```

### What each env needs to add:

Every harness env subclass needs:
- TOOL_NAME: str -- the MCP tool name (e.g., "create_media_buy", "list_creative_formats")
- build_mcp_args(**kwargs) -> dict -- convert env kwargs to flat dict for client.call_tool()
- parse_mcp_result(result) -> BaseModel -- parse ToolResult back to response model

## Implementation

### 1. tests/harness/dispatchers.py -- McpE2EDispatcher

```python
class McpE2EDispatcher:
    """Sends real MCP JSON-RPC through nginx to the Docker stack."""

    def dispatch(self, env: BaseTestEnv, **kwargs) -> TransportResult:
        import asyncio
        from fastmcp.client import Client
        from fastmcp.client.transports import StreamableHttpTransport

        base_url = os.environ.get("E2E_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:8092")
        auth_token = os.environ.get("E2E_AUTH_TOKEN", "ci-test-token")
        tenant = os.environ.get("E2E_TENANT", "ci-test")

        headers = {
            "x-adcp-auth": auth_token,
            "x-adcp-tenant": tenant,
        }

        tool_name = env.TOOL_NAME
        tool_args = env.build_mcp_args(**kwargs)

        async def _call():
            transport = StreamableHttpTransport(
                url=f"{base_url}/mcp/", headers=headers
            )
            async with Client(transport=transport) as client:
                return await client.call_tool(tool_name, tool_args)

        try:
            result = asyncio.run(_call())
            payload = env.parse_mcp_result(result)
            return TransportResult(
                payload=payload,
                envelope={"transport": "e2e_mcp"},
                error=None,
                raw_response=result,
            )
        except Exception as exc:
            return TransportResult(
                payload=None,
                envelope={"transport": "e2e_mcp"},
                error=exc,
                raw_response=None,
            )
```

### 2. tests/harness/_base.py -- add base TOOL_NAME and methods

```python
class IntegrationEnv:
    TOOL_NAME: str = ""  # Subclasses must override

    def build_mcp_args(self, **kwargs) -> dict:
        """Convert env kwargs to MCP tool arguments dict. Override per env."""
        raise NotImplementedError(f"{type(self).__name__} must implement build_mcp_args")

    def parse_mcp_result(self, result) -> BaseModel:
        """Parse MCP ToolResult to response model. Override per env."""
        raise NotImplementedError(f"{type(self).__name__} must implement parse_mcp_result")
```

### 3. Each harness env -- add TOOL_NAME + methods

Example for MediaBuyCreateEnv (tests/harness/media_buy_create.py):
```python
TOOL_NAME = "create_media_buy"

def build_mcp_args(self, **kwargs):
    req = kwargs.get("req")
    if req:
        args = req.model_dump(mode="json", exclude_none=True)
        # Handle creative_ids (excluded by model_dump)
        args = _restore_creative_ids(req, args)  # existing helper
    else:
        args = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k != "identity"}
    return args

def parse_mcp_result(self, result):
    import json
    if hasattr(result, "structured_content") and result.structured_content:
        data = result.structured_content
    else:
        data = json.loads(result.content[0].text)
    return self.parse_rest_response(data)
```

Similar for each env:
- CreativeFormatsEnv: TOOL_NAME="list_creative_formats"
- CreativeSyncEnv: TOOL_NAME="sync_creatives"
- AccountSyncEnv: TOOL_NAME="sync_accounts"
- DeliveryPollEnv: TOOL_NAME="get_media_buy_delivery"
- MediaBuyQueryEnv: TOOL_NAME="query_media_buys"

### 4. Register in DISPATCHERS

```python
Transport.E2E_MCP: McpE2EDispatcher(),
```

## Depends On

- salesagent-10ib (Transport.E2E_MCP enum value exists)
- salesagent-2pnz (E2E fixture infrastructure)
- salesagent-3xyi (proof of concept validates the approach works)

## Files to Modify

- tests/harness/dispatchers.py -- add McpE2EDispatcher
- tests/harness/_base.py -- add TOOL_NAME, build_mcp_args, parse_mcp_result to base
- tests/harness/media_buy_create.py -- add TOOL_NAME + methods
- tests/harness/creative_formats.py -- add TOOL_NAME + methods
- tests/harness/creative_sync.py -- add TOOL_NAME + methods
- tests/harness/account_sync.py -- add TOOL_NAME + methods
- Plus any other harness envs

## Files to Read First

- tests/e2e/test_adcp_full_lifecycle.py -- working MCP E2E pattern
- tests/harness/media_buy_create.py -- existing call_mcp() to understand arg flattening
- tests/harness/_base.py -- _run_mcp_wrapper() for mock Context pattern (what we are replacing)
- src/core/tools/media_buy_create.py -- the MCP wrapper to understand parameter names

## Acceptance Criteria

- McpE2EDispatcher sends real MCP JSON-RPC through StreamableHttpTransport
- Every harness env has TOOL_NAME and build_mcp_args/parse_mcp_result
- At least one scenario runs through E2E_MCP transport
- Real MCPAuthMiddleware resolves identity (not mocked)
- FastMCP enum coercion exercised (string params auto-coerced)
- make quality passes

title: Fix weak assertion: then_webhook_payload_has_metrics — no metric verification
description: then_webhook_payload_has_metrics (uc004_delivery.py:1127) — only checks mb_id appears in delivery IDs list, doesn't verify actual metric fields (impressions, clicks, spend). Fix: extract the delivery entry and assert it has metric fields with non-None values.
notes:
## Prep
- File: tests/bdd/steps/domain/uc004_delivery.py:1137-1143
- Current weakness: Only checks mb_id appears in delivery IDs list; never inspects the delivery entry's totals or metric fields
- Fix: After confirming mb_id exists, extract that delivery entry, assert it has a 'totals' dict, and assert totals contains 'impressions' and 'spend' with non-None values (the two guaranteed metric fields per webhook_delivery_service.py)
- Guard coverage: test_architecture_bdd_no_trivial_assertions (checks for value comparisons vs truthiness-only)
## Verification
- uv run pytest tests/unit/ -x -q: PASS (3841 passed, 19 xfailed)
- BDD guards: PASS (9/9 passed)
- Anti-pattern check: the old assertion only checked mb_id membership in a list of IDs. Now it extracts the delivery entry, asserts it has a 'totals' dict, and asserts 'impressions' and 'spend' are present with non-None values.
- Function reads back clean: yes — no conditional guards, no pytest.skip, no truthiness-only checks

title: UC-003 fix: approval_workflow_autoapproved — update request missing required fields
description:
1 failure (rest only). test_approval_workflow__both_flags_false_autoapproved.

ERROR: AdCPValidationError('Update request must include at least one updatable field (paused, start_time, end_time, packages, budget, push_notification_config, reporting_webhook, context, ext)')

ROOT CAUSE: The scenario tests auto-approval when both flags are false, but the update request has no updatable fields — it only sets the approval flags. Production requires at least one real field change.

FIX: The Given step should include a minimal field update (e.g., budget change) alongside the approval flag configuration. Only fails on REST transport — other transports may handle the validation differently.

FILES: tests/bdd/steps/domain/uc003_update_media_buy.py (Given step for approval scenario setup)

title: BDD fix: then_approval_auto — checks status not approval path
description: tests/bdd/steps/generic/then_media_buy.py:108. Step says 'auto-approved' but asserts status=='completed'. A manually-approved media buy also reaches completed. Must assert the approval mechanism, not terminal status.
design: Assert both status=='completed' AND no workflow_step_id on any response package. This distinguishes auto-approved from manually-approved-then-completed.
notes:
## Research findings

### How auto-approval works in production:
1. Auto-approved: status='completed' in CreateMediaBuyResult, NO workflow_step_id for pending approval
2. Manual approval: status='pending_approval', workflow_step_id IS set on response packages

### How to distinguish auto-approved from manual-approved:
- Check CreateMediaBuyResult.status: 'completed' = auto-approved (or dry_run), 'pending_approval' = manual
- Check response packages for workflow_step_id: absent = auto, present = needs approval
- The event_type in notification: 'approval_required' = manual, 'created' = auto

### Correct assertion for then_approval_auto:
- assert result.status == 'completed' (existing — necessary but not sufficient)
- assert NO package has workflow_step_id set (distinguishes from manual-approved-then-completed)
- OR assert status != 'pending_approval' (simpler, covers the common case)

### Core invariant:
Auto-approval means the media buy completed without a manual approval step — no workflow step was created for human review.

title: Epic: Account Management — complete feature (list_accounts, sync_accounts, admin, resolution)
description:
Complete account management feature per AdCP spec. Separates authentication (Agent/Principal) from billing (Account) with many-to-many access control.

GitHub refs: prebid/salesagent #1012 (data model), #1011 (list_accounts), #1029 (sync_accounts), #1005 (v3 gap analysis)

## Scope
1. Data model: accounts table, agent_account_access table, media_buys.account_id
2. Repository + UoW: AccountRepository, AccountUoW
3. Schemas: extend adcp library Account, SyncAccountsRequest/Response, ListAccountsRequest/Response
4. Tools: _sync_accounts_impl, _list_accounts_impl, resolve_account() helper
5. Transport: MCP + A2A wrappers for both tools
6. Account resolution: wire AccountReference through CreateMediaBuyRequest, SyncCreativesRequest → adapter
7. Adapter: get_adapter() reads account.platform_mappings when account resolved
8. Admin UI: list/detail/approve/reject/suspend/platform-mappings/create
9. Migrations: 3 alembic (schema + backfill + enforce FK)
10. Revert temporary account-optional overrides from adcp 3.10 migration

## Test Obligations
- UC-011 (existing): 45 obligations for list_accounts + sync_accounts protocol behavior
- UC-012 (new): 9 obligations for admin account management
- UC-013 (new): 9 obligations for account resolution in requests
- UC-014 (new): 5 obligations for data migration & backward compat
- UC-015 (new): 4 obligations for multi-agent account access
Total: 72 obligations across unit, integration, and e2e tests

## Design
Full design document: .claude/plans/spicy-juggling-boot.md

## Key Decisions
- Principal stays as-is (conceptually = Agent), no table rename
- ResolvedIdentity unchanged — account resolved per-request from body
- Natural key: (tenant_id, brand_domain, brand_id, operator, sandbox)
- Approval workflow: tenant-level setting (account_approval_mode)
- platform_mappings: copied to Account, fallback to Principal for backward compat

title: BDD: Admin Account Management UI — Gherkin + integration + e2e test pyramid
description:
Establish the BDD testing pattern for admin UI features. This is the template
that all future admin UI testing will follow.

## Why this matters
The API layer (UC-011) has full BDD coverage via pytest-bdd with multi-transport
dispatch. The admin UI has zero BDD coverage — only ad-hoc integration tests.
This task establishes the correct test pyramid for admin features:

  Gherkin feature file → step definitions → integration (Flask test_client) + e2e (Docker + HTTP)

## Deliverables

### 1. Feature file: features/BR-ADMIN-ACCOUNTS.feature
Scenarios covering the admin account management UI:
- View empty accounts list
- Create account via form
- View account detail page
- Edit account fields
- Change account status (approve, suspend, close)
- Status filter on list page
- Validation errors on create (missing required fields)
- Unauthorized access redirects to login

### 2. Step definitions: tests/bdd/steps/domain/admin_accounts.py
Given: authenticated admin session, existing accounts in DB
When: navigate to page, submit form, click button, call JSON API
Then: page contains text, status badge shows, flash message, redirect

### 3. Integration transport: Flask test_client
Step definitions use Flask test_client (no Docker needed):
- Fast, runs in tox -e bdd
- Session auth via test_client.session_transaction()
- Real DB via integration_db fixture

### 4. E2E transport: HTTP requests against Docker stack
Same Gherkin, different transport:
- Runs in tox -e e2e
- Uses requests.Session against live server
- Auth via /test/auth endpoint
- Full stack: Flask + Postgres + nginx

### 5. Test binding: tests/bdd/test_admin_accounts.py
```python
from pytest_bdd import scenarios
scenarios("features/BR-ADMIN-ACCOUNTS.feature")
```

### 6. Conftest wiring
- Detect @admin-accounts tag → AdminAccountsEnv harness
- Transport dispatch: integration (test_client) vs e2e (requests)
- Entity marker: @admin for make test-entity ENTITY=admin

## Architecture decisions
- Feature file is separate from UC-011 (API vs UI are different use cases)
- Step definitions are in domain/ (not generic/) because admin UI is domain-specific
- Harness follows BaseTestEnv pattern but with Flask test_client instead of _impl calls
- E2e transport uses requests.Session (not Playwright) for reliability in CI
- Playwright reserved for visual smoke tests (separate from BDD pyramid)

## Test markers
- @admin @accounts @bdd — for BDD suite
- @admin @accounts @integration — for integration slice
- @admin @accounts @e2e — for e2e slice

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Feature file with ≥8 scenarios
- [ ] Integration steps pass via Flask test_client
- [ ] E2e steps pass against Docker stack
- [ ] make test-entity ENTITY=admin includes the new tests
- [ ] Pattern is documented so future admin features follow it

title: Write Gherkin feature file for admin account management
description: Write features/BR-ADMIN-ACCOUNTS.feature with ≥8 scenarios covering list, create, detail, edit, status change, filter, validation, auth. Follow existing BDD feature file conventions (Background, tags, postconditions).
design:
## Core Invariant
BDD feature files describe user-observable behavior through the admin UI — each scenario maps to a complete user interaction (navigate + act + verify), not implementation details.

## Architecture Decisions
- Tag prefix: @T-ADMIN-ACCT-xxx (distinguishes admin BDD from API UC-xxx BDD)
- No multi-transport parametrize in feature file — transport is a conftest concern
- Background: authenticated admin with tenant, clean DB state
- Status change: test JSON API directly (not JavaScript execution)
- Reuse create_account.html template semantics (shared create/edit form with edit_mode flag)

## Implementation Plan
1. Create tests/bdd/features/BR-ADMIN-ACCOUNTS.feature
2. Feature header with As a/I want/So that for admin user
3. Background: admin authenticated, tenant exists, (optionally) seed accounts
4. 10 scenarios covering: list, create, detail, edit, status valid/invalid, filter, validation, auth, terminal state
5. Use @T-ADMIN-ACCT-001 through @T-ADMIN-ACCT-010 tags
6. Data tables for form field values where appropriate
7. Mark all scenarios @pending initially (step defs come in oj0.1.2)
notes:
## Findings

### Feature File Convention
- Located at tests/bdd/features/, not repo root features/
- Naming: BR-<scope>-<topic>.feature — new scope ADMIN (not UC-nnn)
- Target: tests/bdd/features/BR-ADMIN-ACCOUNTS.feature

### Tag Conventions (from existing features)
- Primary: @T-UC-XXX-name (unique per scenario)
- Category: @main-flow, @alternative, @extension, @edge-case
- Domain: @list, @create, @edit, @status, @auth, @validation
- @pending for unimplemented steps

### Background Pattern
- All existing features use Background for shared preconditions
- Admin background: authenticated admin user with tenant context

### Admin vs API BDD Differences
- No multi-transport dispatch (no MCP/A2A/REST parametrization)
- Steps use Flask test client (integration) or HTTP requests (e2e)
- Given: admin auth + DB setup; When: GET/POST; Then: HTML content + DB state
- Status change tests JSON API directly (no JS execution)

### Blueprint Routes (accounts.py)
- GET /: List accounts (optional ?status= filter)
- GET|POST /create: Create form + submit
- GET /<id>: Detail page (shows status transition buttons)
- GET|POST /<id>/edit: Edit form + submit
- POST /<id>/status: JSON API for status changes

### Status Transitions
- active → suspended, closed
- pending_approval → active, rejected
- rejected → pending_approval
- payment_required → active, suspended
- suspended → active, closed
- closed → terminal (no transitions)

### Scenario Plan (≥8 required, proposing 10)
1. List accounts for tenant
2. Create account via form
3. View account detail page
4. Edit mutable account fields
5. Valid status transition (active → suspended)
6. Invalid status transition (rejected)
7. Filter list by status
8. Validation: missing required name
9. Auth required for access
10. Terminal state has no transition buttons

## Relevant Code
- src/admin/blueprints/accounts.py:24-31 — status transitions
- templates/accounts_list.html — list page
- templates/account_detail.html — detail with JS changeStatus()
- templates/create_account.html — shared create/edit form
- tests/admin/test_accounts_blueprint.py — existing non-BDD tests
- tests/bdd/conftest.py — harness/xfail wiring (needs admin detection)

## CLAUDE.md Patterns
- #6: JavaScript scriptRoot (already used in detail template)
- #2: Route conflict checking (accounts_bp under tenant prefix)

## Risks & Edge Cases
- No existing admin BDD infrastructure — conftest needs T-ADMIN- tag detection
- Admin auth via session (not API token like UC-011)
- HTML response assertions (not JSON like API tests)

title: Delete dead tree code in add_product_gam.html + fix size extraction
description:
Clean up ~350 lines of dead inline tree JS in add_product_gam.html and fix size extraction to not depend on cached tree children.

## Dead code to delete (lines 898-1337)
- openInventoryPicker(type): lines 898-924 — references #inventory-list which doesn't exist in DOM
- loadInventoryTree(searchTerm): lines 999-1064 — orphaned fetch
- renderAdUnitTree: lines 1067-1123 — orphaned render
- toggleTreeNode: lines 1126-1159 — orphaned toggle
- handleTreeCheckboxChange: lines 1162-1165 — no-op
- expandAll/collapseAll/expandToSelected: lines 1168-1243
- searchTreeNodes: lines 1246-1306 — orphaned client-side search
- applyInventorySelection: lines 1308-1337 — references #inventory-list

This code was replaced by the inventory_picker.html component (included at line 2067) but never deleted.

## Size extraction fix
extractSizesFromInventory() at lines 613-727 recursively walks inventoryCache.adUnits to get sizes from selected ad units and their children. With lazy-loading, not all children are in the cache.

Fix: call the existing /api/tenant/<id>/inventory/sizes?ids=... endpoint instead of walking the cache. This endpoint already exists (inventory.py:1264) and returns unique sizes for a set of inventory IDs.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Dead code deleted (~350 lines removed)
- [ ] Size extraction uses /inventory/sizes API
- [ ] Product creation still works (manual smoke test)
- [ ] make quality passes
notes:
## Prep
- File: templates/add_product_gam.html
- Dead code to delete: lines 895-1337 — openInventoryPicker, closeInventoryPicker, loadInventory, loadInventoryTree, renderAdUnitTree, toggleTreeNode, handleTreeCheckboxChange, expandAllTreeNodes, collapseAllTreeNodes, expandToSelected, searchTreeNodes, applyInventorySelection, updateSelectedDisplay
- BUT: openInventoryPicker (898-924), closeInventoryPicker (926-929), loadInventory (931-995), applyInventorySelection (1308-1337), updateSelectedDisplay (1339+) are NOT dead — they're called by the page. Only lines 997-1306 are dead (the tree-specific functions replaced by the picker component).
- Size extraction: extractSizesFromInventory (613-727) walks inventoryCache.adUnits recursively. With lazy-loading, children aren't pre-loaded in cache. Need to use /inventory/sizes?ids=... API instead.
- Approach: Delete lines 997-1306 (dead tree functions). Replace extractSizesFromInventory children-walking with /inventory/sizes API call.
## Execution
- Deleted ~310 lines of dead inline tree JS (loadInventoryTree through searchTreeNodes)
- Replaced extractSizesFromInventory: removed recursive cache-walking, now uses /inventory/sizes API
- make quality: 4103 passed
close_reason: Dead code deleted. Size extraction uses /inventory/sizes API.

title: Integration tests for lazy-loading inventory tree API contract
description:
Write integration tests (TDD Red phase) that define the lazy-loading inventory tree API behavior. All tests should FAIL initially — the backend hasn't been changed yet.

## What to test

### Mode 1: Root nodes (no params)
- GET /api/tenant/<id>/inventory/tree returns only root ad units (parent_id IS NULL in metadata)
- Response includes 'truncated' boolean, 'total_active_count' integer
- Root nodes have 'has_children' flag
- Results are ordered by name (deterministic)
- Stats fields present: placements, labels, custom_targeting_keys, audience_segments, last_sync

### Mode 2: Children (parent_id param)
- GET /api/tenant/<id>/inventory/tree?parent_id=X returns direct children of X only
- Children have their own 'has_children' flags
- Returns empty array if parent has no children
- Returns empty array if parent_id doesn't exist

### Mode 3: Search (search param)
- GET /api/tenant/<id>/inventory/tree?search=term returns matching nodes + ancestors
- Search results are limited (max 1000 matching)
- Ancestors are fetched via batch IN() query (not N+1)
- Tree structure is intact: ancestors wire up as parents of matching nodes
- 'matched_search' flag is set on matching nodes, not on ancestors
- 'truncated' flag present

## Test infrastructure
- Use 'authenticated_admin_client' fixture from tests/integration/conftest.py (depends on test_admin_app → integration_db)
- Create GAMInventory rows directly via session.add() for now (no factory exists yet for GAMInventory — creating one is out of scope but should be a follow-up)
- Create test tenant via Tenant model (minimal: tenant_id, name, subdomain, ad_server, is_active)
- Create hierarchical ad unit data: roots, children, grandchildren with parent_id in inventory_metadata
- Use unique tenant_id per test class to avoid cross-test contamination

## File location
tests/integration/test_inventory_tree_lazy_loading.py

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All 3 modes have tests (root, children, search)
- [ ] Tests run but FAIL (Red phase — backend not changed yet)
- [ ] Tests use authenticated_admin_client fixture
- [ ] Test data creates realistic hierarchy (3+ levels)
- [ ] make quality passes (formatting, linting)
close_reason: 26 integration tests written. 13 pass (existing behavior), 13 fail (new lazy-loading contract). GAMInventoryFactory added. make quality passes.

title: Route admin blueprint queries through repository methods
description:
SEVERITY: Medium (repository bypass, flagged by 4 review agents)
REVIEW IDS: MED-02, AR-02, PP-04, LR-05
FILE: src/admin/blueprints/creatives.py

15+ raw uow.session.scalars(select(...)) queries bypass repositories for:
- Tenant lookups (lines 299, 1080)
- Principal lookups (lines 311, 491, 718)
- Product lookups (lines 343, 1129)
- CreativeReview queries (lines 445, 663)
- ObjectWorkflowMapping queries (lines 122-127)
- CreativeAssignment queries (line 535)

These defeat the repository pattern — tenant isolation is manual per query.

FIX: Add repository methods and route through UoW:
- Add get_principal_name() to CreativeRepository (or shared base)
- Add get_prior_ai_review() to CreativeRepository
- Add get_by_media_buy() to CreativeAssignmentRepository
- Consider expanding AdminCreativeUoW with TenantConfigRepository

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] No raw select() calls in admin blueprint for entities that have repositories
- [ ] All queries go through repository methods
- [ ] make quality green
notes:
## Prep
- Files to modify: src/admin/blueprints/creatives.py, src/core/database/repositories/creative.py, src/core/database/repositories/uow.py
- No transport/impl boundaries involved — this is admin UI blueprint code
- Approach:

### Raw queries to replace:

1. **Tenant lookups** (lines 306-307, 587-588, 766-767, 1093-1094): select(Tenant).filter_by(tenant_id=...)
   - Add TenantConfigRepository or a simple tenant repo. TenantConfigUoW exists but AdminCreativeUoW doesn't have it.
   - Simplest: add a get_tenant() method on TenantConfigRepository and add tenant_config repo to AdminCreativeUoW.

2. **Principal name lookups** (lines 318-319, 465-466, 590, 769): select(Principal).filter_by(...)
   - WorkflowRepository already has get_principal_name(). Can reuse.
   - Add get_principal_name() to CreativeRepository (or a shared helper on AdminCreativeUoW).

3. **Product lookups** (lines 350-351, 1142-1143): select(Product).filter_by(product_id=..., tenant_id=...)
   - ProductRepository.get_by_id() already exists. Add ProductRepository to AdminCreativeUoW.

4. **CreativeReview queries** (lines 557-562, 727-732): select(CreativeReview).filter_by(...).order_by().limit(1)
   - No CreativeReview repository yet. Add get_prior_ai_review() to CreativeRepository.

5. **CreativeAssignment by media_buy** (lines 628-631): select(CreativeAssignment).filter_by(media_buy_id=..., tenant_id=...)
   - CreativeAssignmentRepository exists but lacks get_by_media_buy(). Add it.

6. **ObjectWorkflowMapping queries** (lines 122-127, 142-143): In _call_webhook_for_creative_status
   - WorkflowRepository has get_mappings_for_step(). Add WorkflowRepository to AdminCreativeUoW.

7. **_ai_review_creative_impl_inner** (lines 1093, 1125, 1142): Uses bare db_session
   - This one is tricky — it optionally creates its own UoW. When called with a session, it should use repos instead.

### New repo methods needed:
- CreativeRepository.get_prior_ai_review(creative_id) -> CreativeReview | None
- CreativeAssignmentRepository.get_by_media_buy(media_buy_id) -> list[CreativeAssignment]
- TenantConfigRepository.get_tenant() -> Tenant | None (or new method)

### AdminCreativeUoW expansion:
- Add: products (ProductRepository), workflows (WorkflowRepository), tenant_config (TenantConfigRepository)

### _get_principal_name helper:
- Refactor to use a repository method instead of raw session
## Execution
- Changes made:
  - Added CreativeRepository.get_principal_name(), get_prior_ai_review()
  - Added CreativeAssignmentRepository.get_by_media_buy()
  - Added TenantConfigRepository.get_tenant()
  - Added WorkflowRepository.get_latest_mapping_for_object(), get_step_by_id()
  - Expanded AdminCreativeUoW with products, workflows, tenant_config repos
  - Replaced all 15+ raw select() calls in creatives.py with repository methods
  - Removed _get_principal_name helper (replaced by CreativeRepository.get_principal_name)
  - Cleaned up unused imports (select, Tenant, ObjectWorkflowMapping, WorkflowStep, Principal, Product, CreativeReview, CreativeAssignment)
  - Updated test_blueprint_imports.py (no longer needs select import check)
  - Updated test_cross_tenant_query_isolation.py (5 tests now verify repo usage instead of raw select tenant filtering)
- make quality: PASS (3818 passed, same as baseline)

title: BDD Slice 3: Sync Auth — token required + error variant (5 scenarios)
description:
Vertical slice: auth enforcement in sync + error response + 5 scenarios go green.

## Production code:
- Auth check at top of _sync_accounts_impl (raise AdCPAuthenticationError)
- Error response construction (SyncAccountsErrorResponse)

## BDD step definitions (~9 patterns):
Given: unauthenticated/expired token (2)
When: sync without auth (1)
Then: error variant, AUTH_TOKEN_INVALID, no accounts modified, context echoed (6)

## Scenarios that must go GREEN:
- Sync without authentication
- Sync with expired token
- Error variant — errors present, no accounts
- error_service — service-level failure
- Schema prohibits both_present

## Verification:
uv run pytest tests/bdd/test_uc011_manage_accounts.py -k 'without_auth or invalid_token or Error_variant or error_service or prohibits' --timeout=120
notes:
## Prep
- 5 scenarios: ext-a-no-token, ext-a-expired, atomic-error, atomic-service-error, atomic-both
- Given steps needed: unauthenticated A2A (exists), expired token (new), seller internal failure (new), schema oneOf (new)
- When steps: sync_accounts with datatable (exists)
- Then steps needed: error variant assertions, AUTH_TOKEN_INVALID, errors array, suggestion field, no accounts modified, schema oneOf validation
- Production code: auth error already raised (AdCPAuthenticationError). Error variant response needs to be constructed from the exception in the step assertions.
- Key: ctx['error'] holds AdCPAuthenticationError, Then steps check error attributes (error_code, message)
- atomic-both is a pure schema validation test (no harness)

title: Replace ValueError with AdCPNotFoundError in format_resolver.py
description:
PR #1167 review (ChrisHuie): format_resolver.py:65 raises bare ValueError when a format is not found. This should be AdCPNotFoundError (404) since it represents a genuine 'resource not found' condition.

## What needs to change

src/core/format_resolver.py line 65:
```python
# Current:
raise ValueError(error_msg)

# Should be:
raise AdCPNotFoundError(error_msg)
```

## Implementation notes

- Check .agent-index/errors.pyi FIRST for AdCPNotFoundError signature
- Use ast-grep (not grep) to find the raise site
- Add import: from src.core.exceptions import AdCPNotFoundError
- Update the docstring (line 33) from 'Raises: ValueError' to 'Raises: AdCPNotFoundError'
- This is the format_resolver's 'not found anywhere' path — distinct from AdCPAdapterError (agent misbehaved)
- Run make quality after changes

title: Fix DetachedInstanceError in review_creatives — test failure
description:
SEVERITY: Critical (causes test failure)
TEST: tests/integration/test_link_validation.py::TestLinkValidation::test_creative_review_page_links_are_valid
ERROR: sqlalchemy.orm.exc.DetachedInstanceError: Instance <Tenant> is not bound to a Session

ROOT CAUSE: review_creatives() in src/admin/blueprints/creatives.py accesses tenant ORM attributes AFTER the AdminCreativeUoW context manager closes. The `with AdminCreativeUoW(tenant_id) as uow:` block ends at line 366. Lines 367-374 call render_template() which accesses:
- tenant.name (line 370)
- tenant.gemini_api_key (line 372)
- tenant.creative_review_criteria (line 372)
- tenant.approval_mode (line 373)

After UoW.__exit__ calls session.commit() (which expires all ORM attributes via expire_on_commit=True), these accesses raise DetachedInstanceError.

FIX: Extract tenant attributes into local variables INSIDE the with block before it closes:
```python
# Inside the with block, before line 366:
tenant_name = tenant.name
has_ai_review = bool(tenant.gemini_api_key and tenant.creative_review_criteria)
approval_mode = tenant.approval_mode
```
Then use local variables in render_template().

CROSS-REF: Same class of bug as CRIT-01 in code review synthesis.
REVIEW: .claude/code-review/100326_1413/synthesis.md

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] test_creative_review_page_links_are_valid passes
- [ ] No tenant ORM attributes accessed after UoW closes
- [ ] make quality green
design:
## Root Cause
review_creatives() accesses tenant ORM attributes (name, gemini_api_key, creative_review_criteria, approval_mode) after AdminCreativeUoW closes, triggering DetachedInstanceError due to expire_on_commit=True.

## Core Invariant
ORM attributes must never be accessed after their owning UoW context manager exits.

## Fix Approach
Extract tenant attributes into local variables INSIDE the with block before it closes. Use those locals in render_template().

## Files to Modify
- src/admin/blueprints/creatives.py — extract tenant_name, has_ai_review, approval_mode inside UoW block, use in render_template
notes:
## Reproduction
- Test level: integration
- Failing test: tests/integration/test_link_validation.py::TestLinkValidation::test_creative_review_page_links_are_valid
- Docker required: yes (PostgreSQL)
- Root cause: review_creatives() accesses tenant ORM attributes (name, gemini_api_key, creative_review_criteria, approval_mode) at lines 370-373 AFTER AdminCreativeUoW closes at line 366. expire_on_commit=True means all attributes are expired.
- Data flow trace: GET /review → review_creatives() → AdminCreativeUoW loads tenant at line 300 → UoW closes at 366 → render_template at 367 accesses tenant.name etc → DetachedInstanceError
- Mutation check: if fix is reverted (accessing ORM attrs post-close), test fails with DetachedInstanceError

## FAILED output (last 3 lines):
FAILED tests/integration/test_link_validation.py::TestLinkValidation::test_creative_review_page_links_are_valid - sqlalchemy.orm.exc.DetachedInstanceError: Instance <Tenant> is not bound to a Session
!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! stopping after 1 failures !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
============================== 1 failed in 0.55s ===============================
## Similar Pattern Analysis
- Pattern: Accessing ORM attributes after UoW context manager closes
- Searched: All functions in src/admin/blueprints/creatives.py that use AdminCreativeUoW
- Results:
  - review_creatives() lines 370-373 — THIS BUG (accesses tenant.name etc after UoW closes)
  - _call_webhook_for_creative_status() lines 225-258 — TRACKED as salesagent-647 (accesses step.* after UoW closes)
  - approve_creative() — SAFE (extracts to local dicts inside UoW)
  - reject_creative() — SAFE (same pattern as approve)
  - approve_creative() line 549: media_buy ORM ref escapes but only used in a new UoW scope — SAFE
- Scope: fix original (salesagent-qwi) + already-tracked salesagent-647
## Architect Review
- [LOW] Root cause is correct and well-identified. The fix (extract to locals) matches the pattern already used in approve_creative/reject_creative.
- [LOW] Fix is minimal — 3 local variables, no structural changes.
- [LOW] Regression risk near zero — render_template receives identical values.
- [LOW] Test coverage is sufficient — existing integration test exercises the exact code path.

Overall: ALL_LOW
## E2E Verification: Skipped
Bug is in Flask view code (render_template call) — integration test coverage is sufficient.
